title: Xeridic Tunisian Herbs Tagine
description: A traditional Tunisian dish featuring a rich and flavorful stew made with coconut cream, aromatic herbs, and a hint of spice, finished with a flambe. This Jain-friendly recipe showcases the beauty of North African cuisine.
instructions: Heat the coconut oil in a large clay or ceramic tagine over medium heat.
Add the onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic and ginger and cook for another minute, until fragrant.
Add the cumin seeds, coriander powder, turmeric powder, and cayenne pepper, and cook for 1 minute, until the spices are fragrant.
Add the dried mint leaves and cook for 1 minute, until the mixture is well combined.
Stir in the coconut cream and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, for 20 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened and the flavors have melded together.
Stir in the chopped parsley and season with salt to taste.
To finish the dish, ignite the mixture with a flambe, using a kitchen torch or by carefully pouring a small amount of rum or cognac into the tagine and igniting it.
Serve the tagine immediately, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
Note: To make the dish more substantial, you can add cooked chickpeas, lentils, or quinoa to the tagine before serving.
